samples.gvd:
module pulse_detect #(
    parameter s0 = 2'b00,
    parameter s1 = 2'b01,
    parameter s2 = 2'b10,
    parameter s3 = 2'b11
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output reg data_out
);

reg [1:0] state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        state <= s0;
        data_out <= 0;
    end else begin
        case (state)
            s0: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b1) begin
                    state <= s1;
                end else begin
                    state <= s0;
                end
            end
            s1: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b0) begin
                    state <= s2;
                end else begin
                    state <= s0;
                end
            end
            s2: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b1) begin
                    state <= s3;
                end else begin
                    state <= s0;
                end
            end
            s3: begin
                data_out <= 1;
                state <= s0;
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse Detection Module</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="INITIAL">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="WAIT_FOR_PULSE">Waiting for a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_DETECTED">Pulse detected.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state">State register.</reg>
        <wire id="next_state">Next state wire.</wire>
        
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_PULSE;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                
                WAIT_FOR_PULSE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_DETECTED;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = WAIT_FOR_PULSE;
                    end
                end
                
                PULSE_DETECTED: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = INITIAL;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_DETECTED;
                    end
                end
                
                default: next_state = INITIAL;
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= INITIAL;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        </always_ff>
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic to detect pulses based on input signal transitions
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Initialize state to 'INITIAL' on reset
                state <= INITIAL;
                // Set output to 0 on reset
                data_out <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Update state based on current state and input signal
                case (state)
                    INITIAL: begin
                        // Transition to 'WAIT_FOR_PULSE' if input is high
                        if (data_in == 1'b1) begin
                            state <= WAIT_FOR_PULSE;
                        end
                        // Keep output low in 'INITIAL' state
                        data_out <= 1'b0;
                    end
                    
                    WAIT_FOR_PULSE: begin
                        // Transition to 'PULSE_DETECTED' if input goes low
                        if (data_in == 1'b0) begin
                            state <= PULSE_DETECTED;
                        end
                        // Keep output low while waiting for pulse
                        data_out <= 1'b0;
                    end
                    
                    PULSE_DETECTED: begin
                        // Transition back to 'INITIAL' if input goes high
                        if (data_in == 1'b1) begin
                            state <= INITIAL;
                        end
                        // Set output high to indicate pulse detected
                        data_out <= 1'b1;
                    end
                    
                    default: begin
                        // Default case to handle unexpected states
                        state <= INITIAL;
                        data_out <= 1'b0;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
